taskDescription: Click on the key neuroanatomy terms related to the cerebellum in the text below.
textField:
The *cerebellum* has three functional zones. The *vestibulocerebellum* (*flocculonodular* lobe) controls *balance* and eye movements. The *spinocerebellum* (vermis and paravermal zone) regulates *posture* and *gait*. The *pontocerebellum* (*neocerebellum*, lateral hemispheres) coordinates skilled *voluntary* movements and motor *planning*.

Four pairs of deep *cerebellar* nuclei lie within the white matter. From medial to lateral they are: *Fastigial*, *Globosus*, *Emboliformis*, and *Dentate*. The *Dentate* nucleus is the largest and is associated with the *pontocerebellum*. The cerebellum connects to the brainstem via three *peduncles*: *inferior* (mainly input), *middle* (largest, corticopontine input), and *superior* (mainly output).

Cerebellar lesions produce *ipsilateral* deficits because the output crosses *twice*. Clinical signs include *ataxia*, intention *tremor*, *dysmetria* (past-pointing), *nystagmus*, *dysarthria* (scanning speech), and *dysdiadochokinesia* (inability to perform rapid alternating movements).

overallFeedback.feedback: Review the cerebellar anatomy and try again.
Good effort! Review the terms you missed.
Excellent! You identified the key cerebellar terms accurately.
